MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-18-2018 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT & Notice Pursuant To Ccp 437c
Off Calendar Jun-18-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Hunter Sims III, Deputy City Attorney 415-554-3800 of the Office of the City Attorney for Defendant.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF LOUIS ELDER's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Pursuant To Ccp 437c.

Plaintiff Louis Elder's motion for summary judgment is denied. The motion is both procedurally and substantively deficient. Mr. Elder has not alleged a breach of contract claim and relief on a motion for summary judgment may not be granted on an unalleged claim. As was stated in the April 2, 2018 order denying Mr. Elder's CCP 664.6 motion, Mr. Elder has not shown that the parties entered into a settlement. In particular, Mr. Elder failed to show that the asserted agreement complied with SF Charter 6.102, was signed by the City, approved by SFPD, which is a condition specifically set forth in the asserted agreement.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Not Reported. =(302/HK)

MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT
Off Calendar Apr-02-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF LOUIS ELDER'S MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT pursuant to CCP 664.6 is DENIED. Plaintiff fails to show that the parties entered into a settlement. The settlement does not meet the requirements of SF Charter sec. 6.102 and SF Admin Code sec. 10.22-2. Plaintiff fails to show compliance with the express terms of the Full and Final Release (i.e. SFPD approval) and there has been no compliance with the direct party participation requirement of CCP 664.6. (See Levy v. Superior Court (1995) 10 Cal.4th 578, 584; Sims Dec., par. 6.) The court denies CCSF's request for sanctions. Defendant failed to comply with the separate notice of motion and safe harbor provisions of CCP 128.5(f)(1)(A) & (B). In addition, the court does not find that the motion was "frivolous" within the meaning of CCP 128.5.

ORDER SIGNED.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, email: rhunter@sftc.org.

MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT
Off Calendar Mar-29-2018 Continued To Apr-02-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
